What sort of app or spftware can I build for my latest appointment?

JOB DESCRIPTION
1. Develop and implement a group-wide training and development strategy aligned with business goals
2. Identify training needs across all subsidiaries through skills gap analysis and performance reviews
3. Design structured training programs for technical, operational, leadership, and soft skills
4. Coordinate onboarding and induction programs for new employees across the Group
5. Work closely with departmental leads to ensure training supports operational requirements.
6. Oversee training programs for all the subsidiaries under EIB GROUP with external clients.
7. Supervise and guide Training Officers, Supervisors, and facilitators within the Group
8. Source, evaluate, and manage externaltrainers, consultants and training vendors when reuired.
9. Develop training materials, manuals, presentations, and standard learning resources
10. Ensure consistency in training standards and learning outcomes across all business units.
11. Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of training programs and recommend improvements.
12. Prepare and manage the group training budget and ensure cost-effective delivery.
13. Promote a culture of continuous learning, professional development, and skills improvement.
14. Coordinate leadership and management development programs for supervisors and managers.
15. Support succession planning and talent development initiatives in collaboration with HR.
16. Ensure compliance with regulatory, safety, and industry-specific training requirements.
17. Maintain accurate training records, attendance reports, and skills databases.
18. Introduce modern learning methods, including digital learning and on-the-job coaching.
19. Support change management initiatives through targeted training interventions.
20. Provide regular training reports and updates to the Group Head, Human Resources.
21. Collaborate with HR on performance management and employee development plans.
22. Mentor high-potential employees and support career development pathways.
23. Address training-related performance gaps and recommend corrective actions.
24. Represent the Training function in management meetings and cross-functional projects.
25. Perform any other training and development duties assigned by management in line with the role.



KEY PERFORMANCE AREAS
A. Effective management of equipment and assets attached to the group facilities.
B. Manage the relationship between actual results, budgets, and forecasts
C. Initiate cost-saving model and controls
D. Effective supervisor, motivation, and performance of all admin teams across the group.
E. Development, standardization, and enforcement of administrative policies across all subsidiaries.
F. Timely and accurate execution of day-to-day administrative functions.
G. Smooth communication and workflow between different companies in the group.
H. Centralized purchasing to achieve economies of scale while ensuring quality.
1. Maintenance of accurate, secure, and accessible records.

**Defining the Goal**

I'm currently focused on defining the user's primary goal: receiving a software recommendation for a new job appointment. The role description from the pasted job description is a key factor. I'm exploring how to translate the JD and key performance indicators (KPIs) into specific app features. My objective is to provide an initial foundation for a web application concept.


**Analyzing the User's Role**

I'm now diving deeper into the responsibilities associated with the role, specifically "Group Training and Development Manager." My focus is on translating key performance areas (KPAs) into concrete app features. I'm exploring how the system can assist in skills gap analysis, budget management, and reporting requirements. This analysis will shape the core functionalities of the software, and help determine the user needs.


**Outlining App Functionality**

I'm developing the Minimum Viable Product (MVP). The focus is now on core features: a dashboard for key performance indicators (KPIs), training modules, an employee database, and a budget tracker. I'm prioritizing the AI assistant leveraging the Gemini API for curriculum suggestions, skills gap analysis, vendor email drafting, and feedback summarization. The next phase will be to define features related to assets and facilities.


**Structuring the App**

I'm now detailing the application structure. I've decided on simple state management using `useState`/`useEffect` and a `HashRouter`. I'm drafting the app layout, including components for the Dashboard, Training Manager, Budget Tracker, and AI Assistant. I'm focusing on the AI integration, specifically an "AI Curriculum Designer" to generate training modules based on user input. I've also planned the `geminiService.ts` to handle API calls.


**Defining Feature Set**

I'm now detailing an AI "Skills Gap Analyzer" as a core feature. I'll translate performance data into training recommendations. I'm also planning the step-by-step implementation, including setting up the boilerplate, defining metadata, handling types and mock data (focusing on realistic employee data), and implementing the service layer with Gemini 3 Flash. I'm breaking down the components: Sidebar, Dashboard, Training Module, and AI Planner (a chat interface for users to build curricula). The overall styling will be corporate-themed.


**Refining Technical Design**

I'm solidifying the architectural details now. I've opted for a `HashRouter` for routing, ensuring flexibility if expanded while minimizing dependencies, or a simple state-based route. I'm confirming the Gemini API key integration with `gemini-3-flash-preview` for text tasks. Also, I'll be adding an "Assets" tab to reflect the job description's mentions of centralized purchasing and asset management. I'm focusing on the "AI Strategy Generator" and budget management features from the job description and KPIs to ensure they're prominent.

Based on your detailed Job Description and Key Performance Areas (KPAs), the most valuable software you can build is a **Strategic L&D Management & Intelligence Dashboard**.

This application, which I have named **"Nexus L&D"**, addresses your specific needs by:
1.  **AI Strategy Assistant:** Uses Gemini to help you design training curriculums and identify skills gaps (JD #1, #2, #3).
2.  **Budget & KPI Tracker:** Visualizes budget vs. actuals and training effectiveness (JD #12, KPA B).
3.  **Group-Wide Oversight:** centralized view of training across subsidiaries (JD #6, #10).
4.  **Asset & Admin Tracking:** A dedicated section for tracking facility assets (KPA A).

INTERNAL MEMO

TO: Group Manager (Training)
FROM: Training Supervisor
DATE: 12/02/2026
SUBJECT: Submission of Bright FM Broadcast Training Manual (Version 1.0)
Dear Group Manager (Training),
I respectfully submit for your review and consideration the Bright FM Broadcast Training Manual (Version 1.0), developed from the subsidiary’s existing operational guidelines and expanded into a structured, competency-based training and governance framework.
Following our engagement with subsidiaries on their capacity development needs, Bright FM indicated that its operations currently rely largely on on-the-job training and informal procedures. In response, the Training Unit collaborated conceptually with the subsidiary’s basic SOP outline and has now formalized it into a standardized training manual aligned with Group expectations for professionalism, compliance, operational control, and performance accountability.
The manual currently includes:
Module 1 – Staff Induction & Orientation
Module 2 – Programme Preparation & Presentation
Module 3 – Studio Operations & Equipment Handling
Module 4 – Advertising & Sponsorship Management
Module 5 – Professional Conduct & Ethics
Module 6 – Audience Engagement & Complaints Handling
Operational Appendices (Complaint Log Template, Competency Sign-Off Forms, Ethics Acknowledgment Form, Studio Checklist)
This document is positioned as the foundational training manual for broadcast operations, developed from subsidiary SOPs and structured for standardization, supervision, and measurable competency assessment.
Kindly review and advise on:
    1. Approval for pilot implementation at Bright FM.
    2. Any modifications required to align fully with Group policy.
    3. Consideration for adaptation as a template for other media-related subsidiaries within the Group.
I remain available to provide further clarification or present a brief overview if required.
Thank you for your guidance and continued support.
Yours faithfully,
Antah Benedict Aondofa
Training Supervisor
EIB Group of Companies


EIB GROUP OF COMPANIES
BROADCAST TRAINING MANUAL
Subsidiary: Bright FM Abuja
Version 1.3

MODULE 1: STAFF INDUCTION & ORIENTATION
Module Overview
This module provides structured orientation for all new staff of Bright FM. It ensures employees understand the station’s values, structure, policies, and regulatory environment before assuming duties.
Key Areas Covered:
- Introduction to EIB Group & Bright FM
- Organizational Structure
- Broadcasting Ethics & Regulations
- Workplace Conduct
- Health, Safety & Security
- Training Pathway & Performance Expectations

MODULE 2: PROGRAMME PREPARATION & PRESENTATION
This module standardizes programme planning and on-air presentation to ensure quality, compliance, and audience engagement.
Learning Objectives:
- Develop structured programme outlines.
- Align content with regulatory guidelines.
- Demonstrate professional on-air delivery.
- Manage time and transitions effectively.
- Handle live interactions responsibly.
Core Content Areas:
- Programme planning and scripting
- Editorial compliance
- Voice control and microphone technique
- Time management
- Handling interviews and calls
- Avoidance of defamatory/offensive content

MODULE 3: STUDIO OPERATIONS & EQUIPMENT HANDLING
This module provides structured training on proper use and management of studio equipment. It ensures only competent and authorized personnel operate broadcast systems.
Learning Objectives:
- Identify studio equipment and functions.
- Operate mixing console correctly.
- Conduct pre-broadcast checks.
- Follow studio safety protocols.
- Report technical faults appropriately.
Studio Equipment Covered:
- Mixing Console
- Microphones
- Headphones & Monitors
- Playout Software
- Recording Systems
- Backup Power Systems
- Telephone Hybrid System
Pre-Broadcast Checklist:
- Arrive 30 minutes before programme.
- Test microphones and levels.
- Confirm playlists and adverts.
- Verify backup content.
- Ensure studio cleanliness.
Emergency & Fault Handling:
- Notify engineer immediately.
- Log issues in incident register.
- Activate backup systems where necessary.
- Do not attempt unauthorized repairs.
Assessment & Sign-Off:
- Demonstrate equipment setup.
- Conduct supervised broadcast.
- Complete competency checklist.
- Obtain approval from Technical Supervisor and Training Supervisor.

MODULE 4: ADVERTISING & SPONSORSHIP MANAGEMENT 
This module establishes standardized procedures for managing advertisements and sponsored programmes to ensure revenue protection, regulatory compliance, transparency, and brand integrity.
Target Participants:
- Marketing & Sales Team
- Presenters
- Programme Managers
- Accounts/Finance Personnel
- Station Management
Learning Objectives:
At the end of this module, participants should be able to:
- Understand advertisement approval workflow.
- Ensure payment confirmation before broadcast.
- Properly disclose sponsored content on air.
- Maintain advertising schedules accurately.
- Protect station credibility and compliance.
Advertisement Approval Process:
- All adverts must be reviewed and approved by Management before airing.
- Content must comply with NBC regulations and internal policy.
- Political, medical, financial or sensitive adverts require additional scrutiny.
- No staff member may independently approve adverts without authorization.
Payment & Financial Control:
- Adverts must not be aired without payment confirmation (unless formally approved credit arrangement exists).
- Finance must issue confirmation to Programming before scheduling.
- All transactions must be documented and filed.
- Revenue tracking must align with broadcast logs.
Advertising Scheduling & Log Management:
- Advertising schedules must be prepared daily/weekly.
- Presenters must follow the approved schedule strictly.
- Missed adverts must be logged and reported immediately.
- Make-good arrangements must be approved by management.
Sponsored Programme Guidelines:
- Sponsored programmes must be clearly disclosed on air.
- Editorial independence must be maintained where required.
- Terms of sponsorship must be documented in writing.
- Sponsor expectations must align with station values.
On-Air Disclosure Requirements:
- Use clear statements such as 'This programme is sponsored by…'
- Avoid misleading listeners regarding promotional content.
- Ensure compliance with broadcasting standards.
Risk & Compliance Control:
- Avoid false, exaggerated, or unverifiable claims.
- Reject adverts that may damage station reputation.
- Escalate questionable content to Management.
- Maintain documentation for regulatory audits.
Monitoring & Quality Assurance:
- Periodic review of advertising logs.
- Random spot checks of aired content.
- Revenue reconciliation between Accounts and Programming.
- Continuous improvement of advertising workflow.
Assessment & Sign-Off:
Participants must:
- Demonstrate understanding of advert workflow.
- Accurately complete a sample advertising log.
- Explain disclosure procedures.
- Receive approval from Training Supervisor and Station Manager.

MODULE 5: PROFESSIONAL CONDUCT & ETHICS
This module establishes behavioral, ethical, and professional standards expected of all Bright FM staff. It ensures integrity, accountability, regulatory compliance, and protection of the station’s reputation.
Target Participants:
- All Bright FM Staff
- Presenters & On-Air Personalities
- Engineers & Technical Staff
- Marketing & Administrative Staff
- Management
Learning Objectives:
At the end of this module, participants should be able to:
- Understand professional behavior expectations.
- Apply broadcasting ethics in daily operations.
- Maintain confidentiality of sensitive information.
- Demonstrate respect in workplace interactions.
- Avoid conflicts of interest and reputational risks.
Code of Professional Conduct:
- Maintain punctuality and reliability.
- Dress appropriately and maintain decent appearance.
- Treat colleagues, guests, and listeners with respect.
- Avoid abusive, discriminatory, or inflammatory language.
- Represent the station positively both on and off air.
Broadcasting Ethics Standards:
- Ensure accuracy and fairness in information dissemination.
- Avoid defamatory, misleading, or unverified claims.
- Provide balanced viewpoints where applicable.
- Respect cultural, religious, and social sensitivities.
- Adhere strictly to NBC regulations and internal editorial policy.
Confidentiality & Information Security:
- Protect internal documents, financial data, and strategic plans.
- Do not disclose confidential station information without authorization.
- Secure login credentials and system access.
- Avoid discussing sensitive matters in public or on social media.
Conflict of Interest Policy:
- Declare any personal or financial interest that may influence professional duties.
- Avoid accepting gifts or inducements that compromise objectivity.
- Do not use station resources for personal gain without approval.
Social Media Conduct:
- Exercise caution when posting content linked to the station.
- Avoid statements that may damage station reputation.
- Clearly separate personal opinions from official station positions.
- Obtain management approval before releasing official information online.
Workplace Discipline & Accountability:
- Unauthorized absence may attract disciplinary action.
- Failure to comply with broadcast standards may result in suspension from on-air duties.
- Ethical violations will be investigated in line with HR policy.
- Disciplinary measures shall follow established Group procedures.
Reporting Ethical Concerns:
- Staff are encouraged to report misconduct through proper channels.
- Reports shall be handled confidentially.
- Retaliation against whistleblowers is prohibited.
Assessment & Sign-Off:
Participants must:
- Complete Ethics Acknowledgment Form.
- Demonstrate understanding of code of conduct.
- Sign Professional Conduct Commitment.
- Receive approval from Training Supervisor and Station Manager.

MODULE 6: AUDIENCE ENGAGEMENT & COMPLAINTS HANDLING
This module provides structured guidelines for managing audience interaction, feedback, and complaints. It ensures professional listener engagement, protects the station’s reputation, and establishes a clear documentation and escalation process.
Target Participants:
- Presenters & On-Air Personalities
- Customer Service/Front Desk Staff
- Programme Managers
- Marketing Team
- Station Management
Learning Objectives:
At the end of this module, participants should be able to:
- Engage listeners professionally on-air and off-air.
- Handle complaints calmly and respectfully.
- Document feedback accurately.
- Escalate serious issues appropriately.
- Use feedback to improve programming quality.
Principles of Audience Engagement:
- Treat all listeners with respect and courtesy.
- Encourage constructive participation.
- Avoid arguments or confrontational tone on-air.
- Maintain neutrality on sensitive topics where required.
- Protect the identity of callers where confidentiality is necessary.
On-Air Interaction Guidelines:
- Screen calls before connecting live.
- Brief callers on acceptable language and conduct.
- Avoid broadcasting defamatory or offensive remarks.
- Maintain time control during discussions.
- Terminate calls politely if rules are violated.
Complaints Handling Procedure:
Step 1: Listen actively and remain calm.
Step 2: Acknowledge the concern respectfully.
Step 3: Record details in the Complaint Log Register.
Step 4: Forward to Programme Manager or Management.
Step 5: Provide feedback to complainant where appropriate.
Documentation Requirements:
Each complaint record must include:
- Date and time
- Complainant name/contact (if provided)
- Programme involved
- Nature of complaint
- Action taken
- Resolution status
Escalation Framework:
    • Minor issues: Handled by Presenter/Programme Manager.
    • Repeated complaints: Escalate to Station Manager.
    • Regulatory-sensitive issues: Escalate to Management immediately
    • Legal-risk issues: Refer to Group Management.
Response Standards:
    • Responses must be timely and professional.
    • Avoid defensive or emotional reactions.
    • Where errors occur, acknowledge and correct appropriately.
    • Maintain documentation for audit purposes.
Continuous Improvement:
    • Periodic review of complaint trends.
    • Adjust programming where necessary.
    • Use audience feedback for content development.
    • Conduct quarterly engagement review meetings.
Assessment & Sign-Off:
Participants must:
    • Demonstrate proper call handling in mock session.
    • Complete sample complaint log entry.
    • Explain escalation process clearly.
Receive approval from Training Supervisor and Station Manager.

I'm now zeroing in on defining the architecture for the "Training Programs" module. I'm focusing on the key features: a central repository for submitted manuals, a standardization and approval workflow, and a plan for digitizing the materials into trackable modules within the app. Crucially, I'm considering scalability to accommodate future submissions from different subsidiaries. I'm visualizing a "Program Registry" or "Inbox" view with status tags and source information, and a "Program Detail" view to house module and curriculum content, with actions for pilot approval or change requests.

My key focus is two features: a "Compliance Check" to assess uploaded text against group standards, and a "Digitize" function to convert the text into a structured JSON curriculum. I am ready to begin the implementation phase now. I'm focusing on defining the structures and components to support these integrations.

This is the perfect scenario for the **Training Programs Management** module.

Currently, you have raw documents (Memos, PDFs, Manuals) coming in from subsidiaries via email or physical files. In a digital platform, these need to live in a **"Central Governance Registry."**

Your role as Group Manager is not to write every course, but to **Review, Standardize, and Approve** what the subsidiaries submit.

2.  **Governance Workflow:** You can now see these programs in a "Pending Review" state (as per the Bright FM memo asking for approval).
3.  **Standardization:** It forces the disparate formats (Manual vs. Curriculum) into a unified "Module" structure.

Briech Atlantic Limited of EIB GROUP is the subsidiary company that is primarily involved in the Construction Services, Facility Management, Asset Management, and Logistics Operations.
The Briech Atlantic Limited comprises Architects, Mechanical and Electrical Engineers, Builders, Quantity Surveyors, Electricians, Air Conditioner Technicians, Plumbers, Logistics Officers, Janitors, Drivers, other Facility officers, and Asset Management Personnel that collaborate with field experts and specialized consultants where necessary. STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURES FOR GENERAL OPERATIONS

Purpose
These SOPs guide all staff and management through standardized procedures to ensure professional service delivery. It outlines procedures to ensure operational efficiency, accountability, safety, compliance, and quality across all departments of Briech Atlantic Limited.

Scope
These SOPs apply to all employees, contractors, partners, and stakeholders involved in the company’s core business units:

Building Construction
Facility Management
Asset Management
Logistics Operations






Briech Atlantic Limited operates across four core domains:

Construction Services

Facility Management

Asset Management

Logistics Operations

GENERAL OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES

1. Work Hours and Attendance
Standard work hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Monday to Saturday
All staff must clock in/out daily (manually or digitally)
Overtime must be pre-approved by department heads

2. Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE)
Mandatory PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) must be worn on all sites
Toolbox talks are to be conducted daily on project sites
All hazards, incidents, or injuries must be reported immediately

3. Code of Conduct
Respect for all colleagues, clients, and partners is mandatory
Zero tolerance for corruption, theft, or unethical conduct
Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure agreements must be signed by all employees

4. Reports
Daily Construction unit team reports must be submitted to the Project Manager on or before COB (the close of business)
Daily Facility, Assets Management, and Logistics Operations unit team reports must be submitted to Departmental Heads on or before COB (the close of business)
Weekly Construction, Facility, Assets Management, and Logistics Operations unit team reports must be submitted to the General Manager on or before COB (the close of business)
Monthly operational reports are submitted to the Group Chief Operating Officer
Incident reports must be submitted within 24 hours of any event.

RESPONSIBILITY

Each departmental head is responsible for implementing these SOPs within their team and ensuring compliance. The Quality Assurance (QA) and Compliance team will conduct regular audits to ensure adherence.

SOPs STRUCTURE:

A. Construction Services

A1. Project Initiation
Conduct a feasibility study and site assessment
Estimate the budget and plan timeline
Obtain client approvals and necessary regulatory permits
Appoint a project manager and allocate resources


A2. Execution Plan (Planning and Design)
Assign project supervisor and site engineers
Engage architects and engineers
Finalize design, materials, timeline, and budget
Mobilize labour, equipment, and materials
Submit daily progress reports to management
Conduct risk assessments


A3. Procurement
Identify and vet suppliers
Prepare and issue Local Purchase Orders (LPOs)
Inspect materials upon delivery


A4. Construction Execution
Mobilize the site with safety and logistics setup
Assign work schedules and responsibilities
Monitor progress using project management software
Conduct regular safety/toolbox meetings


A5. Quality Control/Assurance and Handover
Follow local/international construction standards (e.g., ISO, ASTM)
Inspect and test works against specifications
Conduct regular inspections and third-party audits
Perform snagging and de-snagging before final handover
Prepare snag lists and rectify issues
Handover with complete documentation (as-builts, warranties, manuals)


B. Facility Management

B1. Site Management
Conduct site surveys and client briefings
Assign a Facility Staff member to each facility/building
Conduct daily housekeeping and follow a weekly maintenance checklist


B2. Routine Maintenance
Schedule preventive and corrective maintenance
Track service requests and work orders
Ensure water, power, HVAC, and safety systems are functional
Maintain asset register and maintenance logs


B3. Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE)
Perform regular safety audits
Train staff on emergency procedures
Ensure compliance with local regulations


B4. Vendor and Staff Management
Vet and manage subcontractors
Maintain an updated list of approved vendors
Review contracts every 12 months for performance
Clearly define Service Level Agreements (SLAs) 
Conduct regular staff evaluations and training


B5. Diesel Supply

B5.1 Diesel Supply Management

Maintain accurate diesel inventory records (daily usage, deliveries, and consumption logs)
Monitor diesel levels across subsidiary offices using calibrated systems
Ensure diesel storage tanks comply with safety and environmental standards
Authorize only trained personnel for diesel dispensing and maintain logbooks
Implement digital tracking of diesel distribution by vehicle or unit


B5.2 Diesel Supply to Subsidiary Offices

Log all diesel supplied to subsidiary offices and logistics providers
Obtain signed requisition forms and delivery acknowledgments for each transaction
Complete billing and reconciliation monthly through the finance team
Monitor usage patterns at subsidiary offices for audit and reporting


B5.3 Safety and Compliance

Conduct regular inspections of fuel and diesel storage and dispensing systems
Train staff on fuel handling and emergency spill response
Ensure diesel handling complies with fire, environmental, and transport regulations


C. Asset Management

C1. Asset Inventory (Registration and Tagging)

Record assets across all subsidiary offices
Assign unique ID tags/barcodes
Maintain updated asset registers (digital and physical)


C2. Asset Valuation and Lifecycle Management

Monitor procurement, usage, maintenance, and disposal
Conduct periodic asset valuation
Review assets older than 3 years for depreciation or renewal
Track depreciation and lifecycle metrics
Submit quarterly asset reports to finance and operations


C3. Maintenance, Monitoring, and Repairs

Follow a Preventive Maintenance Schedule (PMS) for critical equipment
Log, assign, and resolve emergency repairs within 48 hours
Ensure outsourced contractors meet compliance standards
Maintain warranty and service records
Utilize asset management software


C4. Reporting

Submit quarterly asset performance reports
Flag underperforming or redundant assets


D. Logistics Operations

D1. Fleet & Vehicle Management

Drivers must inspect their vehicles daily and log findings
Routine servicing every 5,000 km or 3 months (whichever comes first)
Maintain logbooks, licenses, and insurance documents
Schedule regular maintenance
Track fuel usage and vehicle performance


D2. Vehicle Servicing

Maintain complete service history for audit and resale purposes
Log all service activities (oil changes, tyre rotations, brake checks, etc.)
Use centralized vehicle maintenance software for tracking and alerts
Ensure vendor workshops comply with company standards



DOCUMENTATION AND REPORTING

All departments must maintain digital and physical records
Daily and weekly internal reports and monthly management reports are mandatory
Incident reports must be submitted within 24 hours.



REVIEW AND AUDIT

SOPs are reviewed annually
Departmental Heads must conduct quarterly internal audits
External audits will be conducted annually


HEALTH & SAFETY POLICY (APPLICABLE TO ALL UNITS)

PPE use is mandatory at all operational sites
Monthly safety drills must be conducted
All accidents must be reported immediately using the Incident Report Form



COMMUNICATION AND MEETINGS

1. Internal Meetings
Construction Unit Team daily briefings at the site (15–30 minutes)
Weekly departmental review meetings
Monthly cross-functional strategy meetings

2. Client and Partner Communication
All client communication must be documented via email or WhatsApp
Formal client reviews are conducted quarterly
Disputes must be resolved within 5 business days


COMPLIANCE AND AUDITS

Internal audits are conducted biannually
External (financial/HSE) audits are conducted annually
The Compliance team will review legal obligations and update the SOPs annually


EMERGENCY PROCEDURES/PROTOCOLS

1. Construction Sites
Evacuation plans must be displayed at all sites and key facility areas
Emergency contact lists are updated monthly
First-aid kits, fire extinguishers, alarms, and exit signage must be visible and accessible
Emergency drills must be conducted every 6 months


2. Vehicle Breakdown or Accident
Drivers must notify their direct or nearest supervisor immediately
Seek medical attention if required; report the incident within 2 hours
Insurance and police procedures must be initiated immediately


TRAINING AND DEVELOPMENT

Mandatory induction training for all new hires
HSE refresher courses every 6 months
Skill upgrade workshops are conducted quarterly




CONCLUSION

All staff are expected to understand and follow these SOPs. Managers are responsible for training, enforcing, and reviewing performance against these standards.








STANDARD OPERATING     PROCEDURE (SOP)
FOR PROJECT TEAM







Briech Atlantic Limited of EIB GROUP is the subsidiary company that is primarily involved in the detailed design
and construction of spatial assets. The Briech Atlantic Limited comprises Architects, Mechanical and Electrical Engineers, Builders, and Quantity Surveyors that collaborate with field experts and specialized consultants where necessary, depending on project scope and requirements. Designs produced by the Design unit are formally presented to EIB Group management before issuance to clients.
The unit has a General Manager, a Deputy General Manager /Project Manager, who is currently supervised by the Chairman of EIB Group.
STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURES FOR PROJECT TEAM
This document contains the Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) for BRIECHATLANTIC LTD Project Team. This comprehensive guide will serve as a roadmap for the efficient and systematic execution of special design and construction projects. As a cornerstone of our commitment to excellence, these SOPs outline the key processes and protocols that define the journey from project initiation to successful project closeout.
The Briech Atlantic Design Unit team plays a pivotal role in translating visions into reality, ensuring that each project is executed with precision, quality, and adherence to industry standards. By adhering to these SOPs, we aim to establish a consistent framework that fosters collaboration, mitigates risks, and ultimately results in the successful delivery of high-quality projects.
These SOPs cover every aspect of our project lifecycle, from the initial project initiation and planning phases to the meticulous design process, efficient pre-construction preparations, and the vigilant oversight required during the construction phase. Emphasis is also placed on the critical elements of quality assurance, client communication, and post-construction support (terms and conditions apply).
As we navigate through this SOP, it is essential to view it not just as a set of guidelines but as a dynamic tool for continuous improvement. Regular reviews and updates will ensure that our procedures remain aligned with the evolving needs of the design industry and the specific requirements of each project.
By adhering to these SOPs, we reaffirm our commitment to delivering exceptional design and construction services that exceed client expectations. Let these guidelines serve as a foundation for collaboration, innovation, and the relentless pursuit of excellence, Briech Atlantic Limited. Together, we embark on a journey of precision, quality, and success in every project we undertake.
PROJECT INITIATION (EIB GROUP MANAGEMENT) 1-2 weeks
CONCEPTUAL DESIGN
        1-2 weeks
DETAILED DESIGN
6-10 weeks
CLIENT APPROVAL
2 weeks
BOQ PREPARATION
        1-3 weeks
PROCUREMENT
1 week (also varies based on the lead times of suppliers)
CONSTRUCTION PHASE
Varies based on project scale
QUALITY ASSURANCE AND CLIENT SIGN-OFF
2 weeks
FINAL DOCUMENTATION
4 weeks
PROJECT HANDOVER
PROJECT INITIATION

Project initiation is the first phase of the project lifecycle, encompassing the processes involved in defining a new project, securing approval to proceed, and assembling the necessary resources and team to initiate the project. This phase is usually executed by the top management of EIB Group, who interface with project clients directly.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

    1. Receive project brief from the client.
    2. Conduct an initial feasibility study and clarify project requirements.
    3. Present feasibility study and finalize project scope with client.
    4. Form a Comprehensive Project Team.

PROJECT TEAM

The composition of the project team could vary depending on the nature of the design project. For instance, an Environmental/Sustainability Consultant, Safety Consultant, or Legal/Regulatory Compliance Specialist could be added to the project team, depending on project requirements.

TIMELINE

        1-2 weeks
CONCEPTUAL DESIGN
The Conceptual Design phase is the stage in the design process where initial ideas and rough sketches are developed. This phase involves exploring different design concepts that are recurrently presented to EIB Group management for feedback and approval. The objective of the conceptual design phase is to ascertain a design concept for further (detailed) development.
The Briech Atlantic Design Unit team will utilize relevant CAD software to produce 2D drawings and 3D models of the proposed design. Physical 3D models or prototypes can be provided as well, if necessary.


STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURES

    1. Develop preliminary sketches and design concepts.
    2. Present initial design ideas to the client for feedback
    3. Revise designs based on client input and finalize the conceptual design.

TIMELINE

1-2 weeks
DETAILED DESIGN
The detailed design phase commences after conceptual design and involves creating comprehensive and specific technical drawings and plans. This phase includes specifying materials, dimensions, and other technical details necessary for construction. The Project Unit will commence this phase based on an approved concept.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

    1. Develop detailed architectural drawings and plans.
    2. Incorporate structural and safety considerations for fortified vehicles and spaces.
    3. Conduct internal design reviews for quality assurance.

TIMELINE

6-10 weeks
CLIENT APPROVAL
Client approval is the formal acceptance and endorsement by the client of the finalized design or project plans. It marks a critical milestone in the project lifecycle and is necessary before proceeding to the next phases of the project. The client’s approval must be confirmed by the liaising party within Briech Atlantic Limited management before the Bill of Quantities is prepared.
If the client demands design changes, Briech Atlantic Limited management will relay information on the required changes to the Project Unit team. If changes require a recourse through another Conceptual and Detailed Design phase, sufficient time to reflect the changes must be granted to the Project Unit team.


STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

    1. Present detailed designs to the client for approval.
    2. Address any client concerns or modifications.
    3. Obtain final approval before proceeding to the next phase.

TIMELINE

2 weeks
BILL OF QUANTITIES PREPARATION
A Bill of Quantities (BoQ) is a detailed document that lists and quantifies the materials, labour, and other resources required for a construction project. It serves as a basis for estimating project costs and is used in the procurement process.
The Briech Atlantic Limited Design Unit team will prepare a BoQ only for the approved design according to present-day market rates. The BoQ prepared must be presented to EIB Group management for approval before procurement commences.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE


    1. Create a comprehensive Bill of Quantities based on the approved design.
    2. Include detailed cost estimates for materials, labour, and other project expenses.
The Briech Atlantic Design Unit team must produce a BoQ of the approved design for review and approval by Briech Atlantic Limited top management.
TIMELINE

        1-3 weeks
PROCUREMENT

Procurement involves the processes of acquiring goods, services, or works from external sources. In the context of construction, it includes obtaining materials, hiring subcontractors, and ensuring the timely delivery of resources needed for the project. The Executive Vice President - Finance and Commercial for EIB Group, through the Accounts and Internal Control Department of Briech Atlantic Limited, oversees the Procurement for the projects.
While the Briech Atlantic Limited Construction team may engage with suppliers and contractors to determine item availability and delivery leading times, Briech Atlantic Limited management must approve and provide funds for the purchase of each item or perform the purchasing of items themselves.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

    1. Initiate procurement processes for necessary materials and services.
    2. Collaborate with vendors to ensure timely and cost-effective procurement.
    3. Obtain necessary approvals for procurement activities.
    4. Issues Local Purchasing Orders (LPOs)

TIMELINE

1 week (also dependent on the lead times of suppliers)
CONSTRUCTION PHASE
The construction phase is the stage in the project lifecycle where physical construction work takes place. It involves mobilizing resources, coordinating construction activities, and implementing the design plans that bring the project to fruition.
Briech Atlantic Limited Design and Construction Unit team will be engaged in supervisory work throughout the construction phase of the designed projects.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE
    1. Coordinate with construction teams to ensure smooth implementation.
    2. Monitor construction progress and address any issues that may arise.
    3. Conduct regular site inspections for quality control.

TIMELINE

Varies based on project scale
QUALITY ASSURANCE AND CLIENT SIGN-OFF
Quality assurance involves adopting processes and measures to ensure that the project meets specified standards and requirements, while Client sign-off refers to the formal approval by the client, indicating satisfaction with the completed work and readiness for project closure.
The Briech Atlantic Limited Design Unit team will supervise the construction works and perform quality assurance works by ensuring that the works align with ISO quality standards. However, top management of Briech Atlantic Limited would also be responsible for ensuring excellent quality of designed works as the general overseers of each project and should conduct regular inspections of the project under construction.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE
    1. Implement	quality	control	processes	throughout construction.
    2. Conduct regular inspections and audits.
    3. Ensure	compliance	with	industry	standards	and regulations.
    4. Document and address any deviations from the project specifications.

TIMELINE

2-4 weeks
FINAL DOCUMENTATION (PRE-HANDOVER)
The final documentation phase involves compiling and organizing all relevant project documentation. This includes ‘As-Built’ drawings, project reports, permits, and any other records necessary for project review, handover, and future reference.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE
    1. Compile comprehensive documentation, including ‘As-Built’ drawings.
    2. Provide clients with all relevant project documentation.

TIMELINE

4 weeks
HANDOVER AND LEAVING THE SITE

Handover is the process of formally transferring the completed project to the client. It involves providing all necessary documentation, ensuring client understanding of the project, and addressing any outstanding issues before concluding the project. This is to be performed by the top management of Briech Atlantic Limited.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE

    1. Complete the handover process to the client and move out of the site.

TIMELINE

2 weeks

Remove and Assets and Admin, clear Budget & Cost, leave it blank, all those dollars value are not needed, this is Nigeria, we spent Naira. 

Also, is there a way we can make it for each manager of subsidiary to see only what concerns them, can you implement RBAC now, and give corresponding codes for implementing using supabase or suggest any other backend and database

**Considering Application Changes**

I'm focusing on the requested application changes. I've decided to eliminate the "Assets & Admin" module completely. Next, the "Budget & Cost" section will be cleared of all existing monetary data, effectively resetting it to a blank slate. Lastly, the planned currency conversion will be the last item to implement.


**Implementing Currency & RBAC**

I'm now implementing the currency change to Naira, updating the UI and data placeholders in the Budget Manager. Next, I'll simulate Role-Based Access Control, focusing on the frontend since I can't interact with a real backend here. The simulation will restrict subsidiary managers' access as requested.


**Simulating Access & Data**

I'm working on the role simulation. I'll add a "Login / Role Switcher" in `App.tsx` (or an `AuthContext`) to mimic UI changes based on selected roles: Group Manager vs. Subsidiary Manager. The filtering of `TrainingManager` submissions and `Dashboard` data, based on the selected role, will be implemented. I will also address the Supabase/Backend question in the conversational part, focusing on schema and Row Level Security. I am updating the data types in `types.ts` to include the `UserRole` and `UserProfile` to handle the auth state. In `App.tsx`, I'll add the "Login/Role Select" and then pass the role to the relevant components and remove the assets placeholder. Finally, I will remove "Assets & Admin" from the `Sidebar.tsx` and conditionally hide "Budget" or "Reports" for Subsidiary Managers based on standard RBAC assumptions.


**Adjusting View Data**

I'm now making final view adjustments. In `BudgetManager.tsx`, I've changed the currency to Naira and replaced mock data with a placeholder message: "No budget data available for FY 2026." For the `TrainingManager.tsx`, I'm implementing role-based filtering, passing a `currentUser` prop. Group Managers will see all submissions while Subsidiary Managers will only see their own.


**Implementing Step-by-Step**

My focus is now on implementing the updates step by step. I've begun with the `BudgetManager` component: removed data and updated the currency symbols. Next, I removed the "Assets" item from `Sidebar.tsx`. I'll introduce a simple `user` state and add a "Role Switcher" to `App.tsx` for easy testing, keeping these changes together.

Here is the updated application.

I have implemented the following changes:
1.  **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)**: Added a login simulation screen. You can now log in as the **Group Manager** (sees everything) or a **Subsidiary Manager** (sees only their specific data).
2.  **Training Manager Updates**: The "Incoming Submissions" list now filters automatically based on who is logged in.
3.  **Budget Manager**: Cleared all dummy dollar data. It now shows a blank state ready for Naira (₦) input.
4.  **Assets & Admin**: Removed this module entirely from the Sidebar and App routing.
5.  **Supabase Implementation**: I have included a new file `supabase_setup.sql` containing the exact PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) policies and table schemas you need to implement this backend securely.

### **Backend & Database Suggestion (Supabase)**

To implement this with a real backend, **Supabase** is an excellent choice because it handles authentication and **Row Level Security (RLS)** out of the box.

**How it works:**
1.  **Auth**: Users log in. Each user has a `subsidiary_id` in their profile (or `NULL` if they are Group Staff).
2.  **Database**: You have a `training_submissions` table.
3.  **Security Policy**: You write a SQL policy that says: *“If user is Group Manager, show all rows. If user is Subsidiary Manager, only show rows where `subsidiary_id` matches the user's ID.”*

The file `supabase_setup.sql` included below contains the code to set this up.

### **Code Changes**

Here is the step-by-step guide to setting up your Supabase backend to work with the "Real" implementation codes I provided.

Since `App.tsx` now uses **Supabase Auth** and **Database**, you cannot just run the frontend; you must configure the cloud backend.

### **Step 1: Create Project & Get Credentials**
1.  Go to **[database.new](https://database.new)** (Supabase) and create a new project.
2.  Give it a name (e.g., "Lumethis") and a strong database password.
3.  Once the project is created, go to **Project Settings** (cog icon) -> **API**.
4.  Copy these two values:
    *   `Project URL`
    *   `anon` `public` key
5.  Paste these into your `lib/supabase.ts` file (replacing the placeholders) or your `.env` file.

---

### **Step 2: Set up the Database Structure**
You need to create the tables that the App is trying to fetch from.

1.  In Supabase, go to the **SQL Editor** (icon with `>_` on the left).
2.  Click **New Query**.
3.  Copy and Paste the exact code below and click **Run**. This enables the UUID extension, creates the tables, sets up security (RLS), and the automation triggers.

```sql
-- 1. Enable UUID extension
create extension if not exists "uuid-ossp";

-- 2. Create Profiles Table (Linked to Auth)
create table profiles (
  id uuid references auth.users not null primary key,
  full_name text,
  role text check (role in ('GROUP_ADMIN', 'SUBSIDIARY_MANAGER')),
  subsidiary text, 
  job_title text,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()) not null
);

-- 3. Create Submissions Table
create table training_submissions (
  id uuid default uuid_generate_v4() primary key,
  title text not null,
  submitted_by uuid references profiles(id),
  submitted_by_name text, 
  subsidiary text not null,
  version text,
  status text check (status in ('Pending Review', 'Changes Requested', 'Group Approved', 'Active')),
  compliance_score integer,
  overview text,
  request_notes text,
  content jsonb, 
  created_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()) not null
);

-- 4. Enable Security
alter table training_submissions enable row level security;
alter table profiles enable row level security;

-- 5. Create Security Policies

-- Group Admins see EVERYTHING
create policy "Group Admins see all"
on training_submissions for select
using ( auth.uid() in ( select id from profiles where role = 'GROUP_ADMIN' ) );

-- Group Admins can UPDATE (Approve/Reject)
create policy "Group Admins update"
on training_submissions for update
using ( auth.uid() in ( select id from profiles where role = 'GROUP_ADMIN' ) );

-- Subsidiary Managers see THEIR OWN subsidiary data
create policy "Subsidiary view own"
on training_submissions for select
using ( subsidiary = ( select subsidiary from profiles where id = auth.uid() ) );

-- Subsidiary Managers can INSERT (Submit programs)
create policy "Subsidiary insert"
on training_submissions for insert
with check ( subsidiary = ( select subsidiary from profiles where id = auth.uid() ) );

-- 6. Automate Profile Creation on Signup
create or replace function public.handle_new_user()
returns trigger as $$
begin
  insert into public.profiles (id, full_name, role, subsidiary, job_title)
  values (
    new.id, 
    new.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name', 
    new.raw_user_meta_data->>'role', 
    new.raw_user_meta_data->>'subsidiary',
    new.raw_user_meta_data->>'job_title'
  );
  return new;
end;
$$ language plpgsql security definer;

create trigger on_auth_user_created
  after insert on auth.users
  for each row execute procedure public.handle_new_user();
```

---

### **Step 3: Create the Users (Your Team)**
Since the `App.tsx` only has a **Login** screen (not a Sign Up screen, as this is an enterprise internal tool), you must create the users manually in the backend.

1.  Go to **Authentication** (icon with a lock) -> **Users** -> **Add User**.
2.  Create an email/password for your account (e.g., `marquis@lumethis.com`, password: `password123`).
    *   *Note: By default, Supabase requires email confirmation. For testing, go to **Providers -> Email** and toggle OFF "Confirm Email" so you can log in immediately.*
3.  Repeat this for **Benedict**, **Anita**, **Mubarak**, etc.

---

### **Step 4: Assign Roles to Users (Critical)**
When you create users in the Dashboard manually, they won't have the "Role" or "Job Title" yet. You need to assign them via SQL so the `App.tsx` knows who they are.
